title: periploca sesseana
intro: Periploca sesseana is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the serpentwood or devil's backbone. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Periploca sesseana include its potential to reduce inflammation, relieve pain, and exhibit antioxidant properties, which can contribute to the prevention of chronic diseases. Periploca sesseana can be prepared as a decoction or tincture for medicinal use, with the root and stem being the most commonly used parts of the plant.
